name: Bhai Rajinder Singh (Singapore)
body: One of the most popular raagis, Bhai Rajinder Singh was blessed with a sweet voice and a knack of composing Shabads to catchy tunes which are still sung by many raagis today. He was also quite adept at singing in raag. Bhai Rajinder Singh lived in Delhi and Bombay for sometime but he will always be known as ‘Singapur Wale’. The reason being that he was so loved by the sangat in Singapore that they did not wish for him to leave. He spent a considerable amount of time in Singapore and Malaysia, during which most of these recordings below were made. Bhai Rajinder Singh’s uncle, Bhai Mehar Singh Ji started his initial training in Kirtan at a very young age and he also received the blessings of Bhai Randhir Singh Ji (AKJ). Bhai Rajinder Singh Ji passed away in Singapore due to a heart condition on 22nd March 1988 at the young age of 32. As they always say, the good ones go too soon. In this case, it is absolutely true. It is very fortunate that some individuals had the foresight to record Bhai Rajinder Singh Ji extensively, leaving us with a huge treasure trove of recordings. In most of these recordings, he is accompanied on the side harmonium and vocals by Bhai Mohinderjit Singh (Delhi). We will be uploading recordings of Bhai Mohinderjit Singh Ji’s own jatha on a separate page in the near future. Please look out for that. If anyone has more recordings or information about Bhai Rajinder Singh, please contact us.
